text.title: Timed Background Service with PeriodicTimer
text.summary: Fixed-interval background work using PeriodicTimer, which cancels cleanly on host shutdown unlike Task.Delay loops.

rule.statement: Use PeriodicTimer.WaitForNextTickAsync(stoppingToken) for fixed-interval work; never use Task.Delay in a loop as a timer.
rule.rationale: Task.Delay ignores the cancellation token after the delay starts if not forwarded explicitly, and loops can leak on shutdown. PeriodicTimer cancels at the next tick boundary cleanly.

antipattern.description: Task.Delay loop used as a timer substitute.
antipattern.reason: Task.Delay does not integrate with the host lifetime cleanly; cancellation after Delay starts requires careful token forwarding. PeriodicTimer is the idiomatic replacement in .NET 6+.
